description: Synthetic policy-level fixtures. Hidden state is available only to the learner oracle and evaluator, never to the tutor policy.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner cannot identify the prerequisite invariant.

scenarios.surface: I followed the step, but why is it allowed?
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner applies a procedure without the prerequisite concept.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner gives a correct rationale but withdraws it while seeking reassurance.

scenarios.surface: Maybe this is right, but I'm probably missing something.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner's proposed reasoning is correct but confidence is poorly calibrated.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner will not commit until the tutor confirms the next move.

scenarios.surface: Should I do the common-denominator step now?
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner knows a viable move but asks permission rather than choosing.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner is solving a related but different target.

scenarios.surface: Why are we comparing them this way?
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner has misidentified what the task asks to compare.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner understands the concept verbally but loses track of symbols.

scenarios.surface: The letters are throwing me off.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner can reason with a concrete example but not the current notation.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner repeatedly redirects questions into requests for the answer.

scenarios.surface: Can you just show me the next line?
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner has enough information to attempt a move but seeks tutor completion.

scenarios.surface: I don't get why that works.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner has a different but valid conceptual model that has not been articulated.

scenarios.surface: That isn't how I was thinking about it.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner's alternative representation is valid and should be elicited.

scenarios.surface: Next I would test the boundary case because the claim depends on whether recognition still holds under pressure.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner is already authoring a productive next move; tutor should hold back rather than add content.

scenarios.surface: My strategy is to compare the two cases first, then decide which relation stays invariant.
scenarios.evidenceNote: Learner has a viable plan and needs protected authorship more than another tutor intervention.
